channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead tree on Hubbard Rd by no 1, 10th April
item.description:
Dead tree next to number 1 Hubbard Rd

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Hubbard Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lack of litter bin in residential parking area leading to frequent littering and antisocial behaviour, 10th April
item.description:
This residential parking area and adjacent green space are regularly affected by littering. There is currently no nearby litter bin, which contributes to waste being discarded on the ground, particularly in and around parked vehicles and along the grass verge. The area appears to attract regular loitering and low-level antisocial behaviour, and the absence of appropriate waste disposal facilities exacerbates the issue. Litter accumulates quickly and is not consistently cleared, creating an untidy environment and potential hygiene concerns. Installing a litter bin at this location would provide a clear and practical disposal point, reduce littering, and support cleaner, safer use of the space by residents and visitors. This issue has been ongoing for some time and is persistent rather than occasional.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Halcot Avenue, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox cub - have bagged body and placed bags in a box, 10th April
item.description:
Live top floor of flats, found dead fox cub on balcony last night on return from work. I have bagged the body and placed in a box and have kept on balcony.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large Tree Branch, 10th April
item.description:
Large tree branch fallen onto the pavement outside 11 Brooklands Avenue.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Brooklands Avenue, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging berry-bearing bush encroaching onto pavement and adjacent garden, posing an irritant risk³, 10th April
item.description:
The bush shown is significantly overgrown and extends beyond its boundary, overhanging both the pavement and the adjacent garden area. It produces large quantities of berries which regularly fall onto the pavement and into the garden. These berries are known to cause skin irritation on contact, and a member of the household has a sensitivity/allergic reaction to them. This creates a recurring health risk when the berries are touched, as well as a slip hazard underfoot when they accumulate on hard surfaces. The overhang into the pavement also presents a public access issue, requiring pedestrians to walk around or beneath the foliage, increasing the likelihood of contact with the irritant berries. This has been an ongoing issue and appears to be worsening as the bush continues to grow. I would be grateful if the bush could be inspected and appropriately cut back to remove the overhang, reduce the risk of contact with irritants, and maintain safe and unobstructed use of the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Halcot Avenue, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 7 DAYS car blocking bus lane, 10th April
item.description:
This car has been parked in the bus lane directly in front of a busy bus stop on Trafalgar road FOR AN ENTIRE WEEK with absolutely nothing done. It is parked in the wrong direction on a double yellow line. Very dangerous for the bus drivers who come by every 3-4 minutes. COUNCIL please act

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Trafalgar Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging trees from council land encroaching into garden, causing excessive acorn fall and blocking sunlight, 10th April
item.description:
The trees adjacent to the property are overhanging significantly into the garden and have been doing so for a prolonged period. They are now dropping large volumes of acorns into the garden, creating a slipping and trip hazard, particularly when underfoot or on hard surfaces. In addition, the density and spread of the branches are substantially reducing natural light into the garden area, affecting its safe and reasonable use. This issue has been ongoing and appears to be worsening over time as the trees continue to grow. The encroachment is now materially impacting both safety and amenity. I would be grateful if the trees could be inspected and appropriately cut back to prevent further overhang, reduce debris fall, and restore adequate light into the garden.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Halcot Avenue, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Accumulating Rubbish, 10th April
item.description:
Rubbish bags left on floor. Attracting foxes and rats that has opened and gone through the rubbish so it's flying everywhere in the neighbourhood. Starting to smell. When it gets hot flies everywhere too.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alderwood Road, Avery Hill
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin and abandoned rubbish bags, 10th April
item.description:
Rubbish all around these public bins attracting pigeons during the day and foxes at night

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish overflow, 10th April
item.description:
Rubbish left outside on floor attracting vermin's and foxes to raid through and the rubbish is flying everywhere in the neighbourhood and my front door. It's been going on for 2 months.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alderwood Road, Avery Hill
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Skips overflowing rubbish, 10th April
item.description:
There are 2 skips in Avenue Road as shops refurbishment. Lots of debris and litter flowing onto road pavement. Pavement marked by these skips. Two bin bags by the council bin near Mays of London and art shop been there 3 days now not been collected by refuge collections. These skips should be covered as dangerous to drivers and pedestrians overflowing!!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pickford Lane, Welling, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
